fresh_facts.claim: The US military seized an Iranian-flagged cargo ship on April 19, 2026 near the Strait of Hormuz after it attempted to violate the naval blockade in the northern Arabian Sea.
fresh_facts.source: US Central Command / Trump administration announcement

fresh_facts.claim: US Navy forces fired on the seized Iranian vessel during the interception operation on April 19, 2026.
An Iranian cargo ship, 'Shoja 2,' transited the Strait of Hormuz from Bandar Abbas despite the US blockade just before ceasefire expiry.
IRGC gunboats fired warning shots at India-flagged tankers and ordered them to turn back, attempting to shut Hormuz access.
Iran's joint military command labeled the US boarding of the cargo ship as 'piracy' and a ceasefire violation, vowing response.
Iranian Vice President Mohammad Reza Aref warned against restricting Iranian oil exports in response to US blockade actions.
Figures linked to Mojtaba Khamenei issued ultimatums to Trump, threatening to abandon talks over blockade and vessel seizures.
Iran announced it has 'no plans' for next US talks in Pakistan, citing blockade enforcement and seizure of vessels as violations.
Iran's Foreign Ministry accused the US of ceasefire violations through blockade enforcement and attacks on merchant vessels.
President Trump threatened Iranian power plants and bridges if no nuclear deal is reached with Iran.
President Trump spoke with Pakistan's army chief Asim Munir about the naval blockade as a hurdle in talks.
Iran briefly announced reopening the Strait of Hormuz on April 17, 2026, but reversed course within 24 hours by firing on a tanker on April 18.
Shipping traffic in the Strait of Hormuz dropped sharply due to dual US and Iranian blockade pressures.
Iran imposed a blockade on the Strait of Hormuz amid US enforcement of its own blockade and seizure of vessels.
Bread shortages, flour lines, and price hikes have worsened 100 days after January 2026 protests in Iran.
The Iranian government retracted aid promises for US-Israeli strike damage to the country.
Iran approved selective global internet access for businesses via 'internet pro' on Monday, April 20, 2026.
An execution was carried out on April 21, 2026 for an individual tied to the January mosque fire connected to protests.
Graves of slain protesters from January 2026 protests have been defaced.
January 2026 protests remain Iran's most widespread and violent unrest in recent history.
Mass unemployment has worsened in Iran due to ongoing military conflict.
US nuclear strikes in 2025-2026 destroyed Iranian nuclear sites at Fordow, Natanz, and Isfahan.
The two-week ceasefire between US and Iran was set to expire around April 21, 2026.
Pakistan is serving as a mediator for upcoming US-Iran nuclear talks scheduled to take place in Islamabad.
Apparent divisions within the Iranian government have empowered confrontational hardliners opposed to US negotiations.

regime_fall.current_status: Following formal ceasefire expiry on April 19, 2026, the US and Iran are engaged in reciprocal military escalations including vessel seizures, blockade enforcement, and threat rhetoric, while Iran has withdrawn from scheduled Pakistan talks and hardliners consolidate control amid severe economic deterioration and internal instability.
regime_fall.last_major_event: US Navy seized and fired upon Iranian cargo ship 'Shoja 2' on April 19, 2026 for attempting to breach the naval blockade, prompting Iran to declare the action a ceasefire violation and withdraw from scheduled diplomatic talks in Pakistan.
